page: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DLSv5 family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 8370C core with hyper-threading enabled at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, and the processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. There is no bundled local storage, though the instance is premium storage capable, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities paired with constrained multi-core and memory performance. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average tier, supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 vector neural network instructions and cryptography acceleration. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, register in the lower tenth percentile due to the physical single-core limitation. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached read and write speeds of 14.32 GB/sec and 13.3 GB/sec respectively.

Given its low-memory configuration and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance represents a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require dedicated processing threads but have minimal memory footprints. It is not suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale multi-threaded compilation, or complex LLM inference. Instead, the D2ls_v5 is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic single-threaded scripting, small-scale Redis operations, or simple background tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v5 is a low-memory, x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 2 vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading, supported by 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable. Benchmark results show average single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles due to the single physical core constraint. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, simple background scripting, and low-intensity utility workloads that do not require substantial memory or parallel processing capabilities.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v5 is a low-memory instance featuring 2 vCPUs on a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v5 is a low-memory virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vcpu virtual machine with a low-memory footprint designed for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz
2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Average single-core performance with AVX-512 instruction support
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded scripting
